How did you hear about us?

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Reference DOM (Multiple select skills): Skills field uses select multiple for multi-value selection Reference DOM (Table layout form): Form laid out as HTML table with labels in td cells Reference DOM (Other option reveal): Referral source Other checkbox reveals free-text input Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: decoy-form, shadow-host, table-layout, custom-combobox, split-phone. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: table rows with inputs in td cells. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Multiple select skills; Table layout form; Other option reveal.
